source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, China/Taiwan fabs, global warming (China's Not Getting Taiwan's Fabs)
summary: Humanoid robots will be ubiquitous in homes and factories within 10 years
summary_extended: Baker predicts that humanoid robots will be ubiquitous in both homes and factories within a 10-year timeframe, framing this as a high-conviction long-term structural view. He cites the Optimus robot (Tesla's humanoid) mining asteroids as an even longer-term vision, suggesting he sees the trajectory of humanoid robotics extending well beyond near-term industrial use cases. The thesis is presented alongside a prediction about the arrival of artificial superintelligence (ASI), implying Baker views these as complementary mega-trends in the coming decade. However, Baker does not name specific investable companies or provide a detailed mechanism for how to express this view, making the investment implication directionally clear (long humanoid robotics exposure) but not precisely actionable from this excerpt alone.
detail: Baker's 10-year prediction includes humanoid robots everywhere — in houses doing useful things and in factories. He mentions Optimus robots mining asteroids as a longer-term vision. This is framed as a high-conviction 10-year prediction alongside ASI.
confidence_evidence: Stated as a 10-year prediction with conviction but without specific investment names or detailed mechanism
quote_fragment: humanoid robots will be everywhere in houses and factories

ticker_audit.reasoning: Baker explicitly references the Optimus robot, which is Tesla's humanoid robot program, making TSLA a clear and unambiguous direct beneficiary of this thesis. This is the only US-listed humanoid robotics company with a named product in the thesis.
